content: We can design biologically aligned retrieval-augmented agent architectures that mirror the prefrontal-hippocampal memory retrieval system to enable autonomous curiosity-driven active learning. In this framework, the hippocampus corresponds to the external vector knowledge base in retrieval-augmented generation, while the prefrontal cortex corresponds to the agent's policy module that actively selects which information to retrieve based on the agent's current uncertainty and curiosity.
